TF-IDF.sentence: The project uses TF-IDF vectorization to extract features from textual data and Logistic Regression for classification.
The process includes dataset analysis, text preprocessing, feature extraction using TF-IDF, and classification using Logistic Regression.
To convert textual data into numerical features using TF-IDF Vectorization.
Feature Extraction- Use TF-IDF Vectorizer to convert text into numerical representations.
Model Training - Train the Logistic Regression classifier on processed TF-IDF features.
Despite its simplicity, Logistic Regression can perform very well when combined with proper feature extraction techniques like TF-IDF.
In this project, Logistic Regression was trained on TF-IDF vectorized text data, which captures the importance of words across the dataset.

TF-IDF Vectorizer (Feature Extraction): Although not a learning algorithm by itself, the TF-IDF (Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency) vectorizer plays a crucial role in converting text data into numerical form so that machine learning models can work with it.
In the context of fake news detection, TF-IDF helps highlight key differences in word usage between fake and real articles.
The use of TF-IDF Vectorization helped highlight important words and patterns in the text, allowing the model to better distinguish between fake and real news based on word frequency and relevance.

The project demonstrates how effective text preprocessing and feature extraction methods like TF-IDF, combined with a classification model such as Logistic Regression, can accurately distinguish between real and fake news articles.

machine learning.sentence: Data Science Mini Project Report On Fake News Detector Using Machine Learning (A.Y 2024-2025) PREPARED BY Under the guidance of Dr.

Vinod Sapkal Assistant Professor Department of Information Technology Vasantdada Patil Pratishthan's College of Engineering & Visual Arts UNIVERSITY OF MUMBAI (AY 2025-26) CERTIFICATE This is to certify that the project entitled “Fake News Detector Using Machine Learning” is bonafide work of Anand Dane vu4f2223094, Swaroop Sawant vu4f2223100, Omkar Patil vu4f2223108, Prathamesh Thakare vu4f2223103, submitted to University of Mumbai in partial fulfillment of requirement for the award of degree of “Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology-Semester-VII” Dr.
This project focuses on developing a Fake News Detection System using Machine Learning techniques.
Therefore, developing an automated system using Machine Learning to detect fake or misleading information has become essential.
PROPOSED SYSTEM The proposed Fake News Detection System uses supervised machine learning methods to classify news articles as real or fake.
By combining both fake and real articles, this dataset allows us to train a machine learning model to detect patterns in the text that can help identify misinformation.

TF-IDF Vectorizer (Feature Extraction): Although not a learning algorithm by itself, the TF-IDF (Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency) vectorizer plays a crucial role in converting text data into numerical form so that machine learning models can work with it.
Conclusion: Thus, we have successfully built and implemented a Fake News Detection system using machine learning techniques.
